subject: integrate artifact broker into multicloud plugins
commit_message:
integrate artifact broker into multicloud plugins

add config.json for each multicloud plugin
update deployment to add it as sidecar
update the values.yaml of multicloud to store image name
remove the hardcode namespace
fix typo and use 1.3.1 image tag

Change-Id: I6cc9e381ea655b1d75760b013d2be693b0c0575c
Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-597
Signed-off-by: liboNet <libo.zhu@intel.com>

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

> (1 comment)

hello, Alexis,
      thanks for your comment. there may be some misunderstanding,
since the artifactImage is used by several subchart like multicloud-starling and multicloud-windriver, which to be used by more later.
      if use the method as you mention:
image:
   framework: XYZ
   artifactborker: XYZ

     such artifactborker can NOT be passed to sub chart, it will report such error:
"[ERROR] templates/: render error in "multicloud/charts/multicloud-windriver/templates/deployment.yaml": template: multicloud/charts/multicloud-windriver/templates/deployment.yaml:99:61: executing "multicloud/charts/multicloud-windriver/templates/deployment.yaml" at <.Values.image.artifa...>: can't evaluate field artifactbroker in type interface {}"

     btw, such artifact broker image are supposed to be used by several sub charts and acts as sidecar. if can NOT be put into the multicloud/values.yaml, i'm afraid need add such definition into each subchart's values.yaml. 

     how do you think?

Patch Set 5:

>      btw, such artifact broker image are supposed to be used by several sub charts and acts as sidecar. if can NOT be put into the multicloud/values.yaml, i'm afraid need add such definition into each subchart's values.yaml. 

Ah, then what you've done is fine
